concerns.text: T008 (taxonomy_builder.py) generates centroids but lacks a preceding task to fetch/parse the AgentDoG 1.5 taxonomy source file. T013a (drift_scoring.py) consumes these centroids. Without a 'fetch_taxonomy' task, the producer (T008) has no input data, breaking the artifact flow.

T021a (validation.py) requires 'human-annotated CSV' as input to merge with drift scores. No task exists to generate this ground truth (US-02 requires human annotation). T021a is a consumer of an artifact that is never produced by any task in the list.

T028 relies on external API (gpt-4o-mini) for US-03 comparison. This violates the 'Reproducibility' principle (Constitution Principle I) which mandates results be reproducible by re-running code against data without external dependencies. The task order implies a dependency on an external, non-deterministic service that breaks the artifact flow for a research tool.

T014 implements the logic for empty logs but omits the explicit step to 'flag' the record in the output CSV as required by the spec's Edge Cases. The artifact (CSV) will be produced, but it will lack the required 'flag' column/indicator, making the output incomplete for the downstream manual review workflow.
